stem: A IT operations data backup uses 3 parallel storage devices for redundancy. The data recoverable requires at least one storage device to function. storage device 1 has a reliability between 69% and 87% (uncertain due to limited operational data). storage device 2 has a reliability of 78%. storage device 3 has a reliability of 86%. What is the overall probability that the data recoverable?

ground_truth.answer: P(data recoverable) ranges from 0.9905 to 0.996. Point estimate: 0.9932.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 storage devices fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.31 × 0.22 × 0.14 = 0.0095. P(works) = 0.9905.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.13 × 0.22 × 0.14 = 0.004. P(works) = 0.996.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
